sourceMovie.title: Balls Up
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Balls Up (2026) as a comedy theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The comedy and action blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Rundown
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Balls Up clicked for you, The Rundown (2003) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and action, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Balls Up?
faq.answer: Try The Rundown, Accident Man, That Man from Rio—each shares DNA with Balls Up while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Balls Up?
faq.answer: Balls Up (2026) is best described as Comedy, Action, Adventure. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Balls Up worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 5.6/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
